explanation: The phrase 'Start-up Mode' maps to the atomic proposition 'StartUpMode'. The requirement 'The controller shall check the communication of the controller with the sensors and valves' is broken down: 'check the communication of the controller with the sensors' maps to 'checkCommsSensors', 'and' maps to the '&' operator, and 'valves' (implying checking communication with valves) maps to 'checkCommsValves'. Combining these, the action is 'checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves'. The word 'shall' in a requirement context implies that this action must eventually occur. So, 'F (checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves)'. The overall statement 'Start-up Mode: The controller shall check...' means that globally, whenever the system is in Start-up Mode, this check must eventually be performed. This leads to the LTL formula G(StartUpMode -> F(checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves)).
